mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% (CPIAUCSL) signals central bank inaction, leading to real rate compression and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors price in higher discount rates.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 255 despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates robust supply-side capacity utilization, driving positive S&P 500 returns through improved corporate earnings expectations over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint 5-day return of INDPRO and PAYEMS fails to correlate with SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.05) across three consecutive 6-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: A labor market tightening phase (UNRATE < 4%) combined with rising wage growth (PAYEMS) triggers inflationary expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as central banks preemptively tighten policy.
failure_condition: SPX records positive 60-day returns in >50% of periods where UNRATE < 4% and PAYEMS > 150k.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts (HOUST) reflects strong consumer confidence and demand-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through improved corporate revenue outlooks.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain negative in 70%+ of periods with HOUST growth exceeding 2%.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in consumer credit availability (RSAFS) combined with weak housing demand (HOUST) signals reduced private sector leverage, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as risk appetite recovers.
failure_condition: The joint 60-day return of RSAFS and HOUST fails to correlate with SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.05) across three consecutive 6-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) during a monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS > 3%) signals overcapacity correction, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns remain positive in >50% of periods where INDPRO declines and FEDFUNDS > 3%.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP contraction (GDPC1) paired with rising unemployment (UNRATE) indicates economic slowdown, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through reduced corporate earnings and risk-off sentiment.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns remain positive in >50% of periods where GDPC1 declines and UNRATE exceeds 3.5%.
